primary_sources.title: RFC 8259: The JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) Data Interchange Format
primary_sources.finding: JSON parsers may set limits on accepted text size and maximum nesting depth.

repository_context.gap: The recursive scanner rejected duplicate members but had no explicit byte-size or nesting-depth contract.

finding: A fail-closed evidence ingestion boundary needs deterministic resource limits as well as semantic validation. Otherwise adversarially large or deeply nested JSON can make availability and failure behavior runtime-dependent.

design_implication.depth_definition: Each entered object or array increments depth by one.
design_implication.configuration_policy: Overrides must be positive safe integers.
design_implication.compatibility: Existing callers retain the same default function signatures; optional limits are additive.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical implementation and dependency copies

uncertainty: Default limits are policy choices, not values mandated by RFC 8259.
Repository-wide CI has not yet executed the new tests.
JSON.parse and canonical serialization may have lower runtime-specific limits after the scanner accepts input.
falsification_route: Produce an input deeper than maxDepth that reaches JSON.parse instead of being rejected by the scanner.
Produce a UTF-8 input larger than maxBytes that is accepted.
Show a valid input at the configured boundary being rejected.
Show an existing duplicate-member adversarial case becoming accepted under custom limits.
trust_limit: Passing resource and syntax gates does not prove source authenticity, semantic truth, signature validity, or builder trust.
privacy_boundary: No private chat, health data, credentials, or unreviewed user files were inspected or published.
